title: Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program 2026–2027 — Comprehensive Guide for Applicants
summary: The Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program (2026–2027) is a highly supportive academic preparation initiative designed for aspiring graduate students who plan to apply to AI‑related Master’s and Ph.D. programs during the 2026–2027 admissions cycle. This program is hosted by Black in AI and aims to equip applicants with the knowledge, structure, mentorship, and community needed to submit strong and competitive graduate school applications. The application deadline for the program is June 21, 2026 (Anywhere on Earth — AoE) . Program Overview The Emerging Leaders in AI Graduate Prep Program is built for individuals who intend to apply to graduate programs in fields connected to artificial intelligence. These may include computer science, machine learning, data science, robotics, computational neuroscience, and other AI‑adjacent disciplines. Participants must be planning to submit their graduate school applications between October 2026 and March 2027 . The program does not fund application‑related expenses such as GRE fees, TOEFL fees, or university application fees, so applicants must have a plan to cover these costs independently. What the Program Offers The program provides a wide range of structured support resources to help applicants prepare competitive graduate school applications. These include: 1. Graduate Application Support Materials Participants receive access to curated documents, guides, and resources that explain: How to structure a strong Statement of Purpose How to request impactful recommendation letters How to build a compelling CV for graduate school How to identify suitable programs and advisors 2. Community‑Based Peer Engagement The program fosters a collaborative environment where participants can: Connect with peers applying to similar programs Share insights, challenges, and strategies Build long‑term academic and professional networks 3. Facilitated Large‑Group Sessions These sessions are designed to help applicants understand: The graduate admissions process How to prepare competitive applications How to manage timelines and deadlines How to navigate program selection 4. One‑to‑One Mentorship Opportunities Participants may receive personalized mentorship from experienced scholars and professionals in AI. Mentorship may include: Reviewing application materials Providing feedback on research interests Offering guidance on program selection Preparing for interviews 5. Application Fee Waiver Verification The program may assist with: Membership verification for institutions that offer fee waivers Guidance on how to request fee waivers from universities 6. Mock Interview Preparation Participants may receive: Practice interviews Feedback on communication and research articulation Guidance on how to present academic and professional experiences 7. Workshop Speaker Series The program hosts expert speakers who cover topics such as: Research in AI Graduate school expectations Academic career pathways Industry and research lab opportunities 8. Online Graduate Application Information Session This session provides: A step‑by‑step overview of the application process Insights into what admissions committees look for Tips for strengthening each component of the application Important Notes for Applicants The program does not cover GRE, TOEFL, or graduate application fees. Applicants must have a plan to cover all personal application‑related expenses. All required materials must be submitted by June 21, 2026 (AoE) . Late submissions will not be accepted, and no extensions will be granted. Questions may be directed to academic@blackinai.org . How to Apply Applicants must complete the official application form and upload all required documents through the designated submission portal. The form will automatically record the applicant’s name, email, and photo associated with their Google account when files are uploaded. For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post Emerging Leaders in AI: Graduate Prep Program 2026–2027 — Comprehensive Guide for Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fulbright Transatlantic Diplomacy Program (TAD) for German MPP Students at the University of Erfurt
summary: The Fulbright Transatlantic Diplomacy Program (TAD) is one of the most prestigious international scholarship pathways available to German Master of Public Policy (MPP) students at the Willy Brandt School of Public Policy, University of Erfurt. This comprehensive program—offered by Fulbright Germany—integrates academic study in the United States, leadership development, professional training, and long-term transatlantic networking. It is uniquely structured to allow students to continue their MPP studies seamlessly while gaining international exposure at top U.S. policy schools. Overview of the Fulbright Transatlantic Diplomacy Program The TAD scholarship is designed exclusively for German citizens admitted to the MPP program at the Brandt School. It enables selected students to spend one or two academic terms in the United States, participate in professional development activities, and join the global Fulbright community. The program is fully aligned with the MPP curriculum, ensuring that students remain within the standard period of study. Key Features of the TAD Scholarship 1. Eligibility and Application Timing Applicants must be German citizens already admitted to the MPP program at the University of Erfurt. Applications are submitted before the start of the MPP—during the summer prior to enrollment. This early timeline ensures that students benefit from the scholarship’s training and networking components from the very beginning of their studies. 2. Duration of Study in the United States Students may spend 4 to 9 months abroad. This corresponds to one or two academic terms at a partner university. The program includes the option to complete up to two months of an internship during the stay. 3. Comprehensive Funding Package Fulbright Germany provides substantial financial and administrative support, including: Full coverage of U.S. tuition fees for all TAD participants. Funding for living expenses and enrollment-related fees: USD 13,800 for 4 months Up to USD 31,000 for 9 months A J‑1 visa issued free of charge. Participation in Fulbright’s ASPE health coverage (subject to host university acceptance). 4. Academic and Professional Integration Students may be able to complete their mandatory MPP internship in the United States after their study term, allowing them to combine academic and practical experience. The program includes access to: Leadership training Professional development workshops Networking with transatlantic organizations and policy stakeholders 5. Access to the Global Fulbright Network Participants join a worldwide community of scholars, policymakers, and professionals. This network provides: Long-term career connections Opportunities for collaboration Access to Fulbright alumni events and resources Partner Universities in the United States TAD scholars may study at one of several top-tier U.S. institutions known for excellence in public policy and international affairs: George Washington University – Elliott School of International Affairs (Washington, D.C.) University of Denver – Josef Korbel School of Global and Public Affairs University of Pittsburgh – School of Public and International Affairs University of Washington, Seattle – Henry M. Jackson School of International Studies University of Washington, Seattle – Evans School of Public Policy and Governance Each partner institution offers a unique academic environment, strong faculty expertise, and access to influential policy networks. How to Apply Application Deadline 31 August 2026 This deadline applies to students beginning their MPP in October 2026 and planning to go abroad in Fall 2027. Notification Timeline Fulbright Germany will notify selected candidates around mid‑December 2026 . Application Support The Brandt School strongly recommends contacting the program coordinators before submitting your application. Early consultation helps you: Identify the most suitable partner university Develop a strong and coherent study plan Understand the academic and professional expectations of the program Expert Endorsement Professor Andreas Goldthau, Director of the Brandt School and a Fulbright Alumnus, emphasizes the transformative nature of the TAD scholarship: “Fulbright is an experience for life, and winning it clearly is a testament to personal excellence.” Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities here The post Fulbright Transatlantic Diplomacy Program (TAD) for German MPP Students at the University of Erfurt appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Funded European Union Delegation Traineeship 2026 at the Council of Europe for Young Graduates
summary: Key Details Opportunity: Funded Traineeship for Young Graduates Host: EU Delegation to the Council of Europe Location: Strasbourg, France Duration: Up to 6 Months Start Date: September 2026 (Tentative) Application Deadline: June 4, 2026 Funding: Monthly grant for living expenses Eligible Fields: International Relations, Law, Political Science, Public Administration, Communications, and related fields Languages Required: English (C2) and French (C1) Experience Requirement: Less than 1 year of professional experience after graduation Apply for the Funded EU Delegation to the Council of Europe Traineeship 2026 The deadline for submitting applications is 04/06/2026 Are you a young graduate interested in international relations? Are you curious to learn more about work of a diplomatic mission and how the EU Delegation represents EU interests and values in Council of Europe? What is offered: A traineeship of up to 6 months in 2026 within the Head of Delegation Section of the EU Delegation to the Council of Europe,. The tentative start date is in September 2026. Main tasks : Follow key dossiers of the Committee of Ministers, the Parliamentary Assembly (PACE), relevant Rapporteur Groups and expert groups; Participate in the Committee of Ministers meetings, rapporteur groups and expert groups meetings, preparing summaries and reporting; Follow the jurisprudence and other activities of the ECtHR, the reform of the ECtHR, the cooperation between ECtHR and Court of Justice of European Union (CJEU), and the EU accession to the ECtHR and other CoE Conventions; Drafting meeting minutes, speak points, briefings and notes; Researching and preparing documents and notes; Drafting, proof-reading and editing documents; Providing support in the daily management of EUDEL activities (events, receptions, working lunches, meetings, logistic, etc.). Qualifications or special requirements : Bachelor degree (or higher) in a relevant field (political science, international relations, public administration, law, communication etc.) or similar interest in these fields Candidates must have less than one year of professional experience after their graduation by the signing of the Traineeship agreement . (other traineeships or student jobs or volunteering works carried out during university or after graduation are not considered as professional experience) Ability and willingness to work in an international environment; Proficiency in both English (C2) and French (C1); Very good analytical and drafting skills; Very good organisational skills, ability to deliver good quality work in tight deadlines and in a high-pressure environment; Proactivity, ability to work in a team as well as independently; Computer literacy with practical experience in the use of Microsoft packages. How to apply? Candidates must apply to the e-mail address delegation-strasbourg-trainees@eeas.europa.eu and send the following documents: A detailed standard Europass curriculum vitae ( CV ) https://europass.cedefop.europa.eu/ (CVs in other format will not be considered) a cover letter describing why you want to participate in a EU traineeship and an application form Please mention in the subject of your e-mail : Funded Traineeship – HoD Section Important information to read before applying Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship). Each selected trainee will receive a monthly grant to cover living expenses. All costs related to travels, visa, insurance, accommodation must be borne by the trainee. Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered. After the evaluation of all applications, shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview. Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email. In case the selected candidates are not able to present the required documents, their application will be rejected. The traineeships can only be offered in compliance with the local legislation as well as the legislation of the country of origin. Citizens from EU Member states: must provide proof that they will be covered against the risk of a pandemic and the costs of repatriation; must hold a relevant entrance visa and / or temporary residence permit where applicable, based on the traineeship agreement, the duration of the traineeship, and in compliance with the local legislation. Source: https://www.eeas.europa.eu/delegations/council-europe/funded-traineeship-young-graduates-eu-delegation-council-europe-2_en The post Funded European Union Delegation Traineeship 2026 at the Council of Europe for Young Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research Graduate Professional Opportunity: Middle East WMD‑Free Zone Project (2026 Intake)
summary: Deadline: 4 June 2026 The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) has opened applications for its Graduate Professional (Middle East WMD‑Free Zone) position—an exceptional entry point for emerging researchers, analysts, and peace and security professionals seeking hands‑on experience within the UN system. This six‑month, full‑time role (with the possibility of extension) offers the opportunity to contribute directly to one of the world’s most complex and strategically significant disarmament initiatives. This article provides a comprehensive, high‑detail overview of the role, responsibilities, eligibility criteria, stipend structure, and application process to help prospective candidates prepare a strong submission. About UNIDIR The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research is a voluntarily funded, autonomous UN institute headquartered in Geneva. As one of the few global policy institutes dedicated exclusively to disarmament and international security, UNIDIR plays a critical role in: Generating evidence‑based research Facilitating dialogue among states, experts, and civil society Supporting the development of innovative, practical solutions to global security challenges The Institute’s work spans multiple thematic areas, including weapons of mass destruction, space security, conventional weapons, gender and disarmament, and emerging technologies. About the Middle East WMD‑Free Zone Project The Graduate Professional will join UNIDIR’s Middle East Weapons of Mass Destruction Free Zone (ME WMDFZ) Project , which supports the regional initiative to establish a zone free of nuclear, chemical, and biological weapons in the Middle East. The project promotes four core components essential to the future of the Zone: Non‑proliferation Disarmament Peaceful cooperation Verification and safeguards The selected candidate will contribute to research, policy engagement, and programmatic activities that advance these objectives. Position Overview Position Title: Graduate Professional Location: Geneva or Remote Starting Date: 1 September 2026 (Negotiable) Occupational Rate: 100% (Negotiable) Duration: 6 months, with potential extension Deadline: 4 June 2026 (midnight Geneva time) Stipend Graduate Professionals receive a modest monthly stipend as a contribution to living expenses: USD 1331 for in‑person placements in Geneva USD 665.5 for remote placements Candidates funded by another institution will have their stipend topped up to match the above amounts. Important: All costs related to travel, visas, accommodation, health insurance, and living expenses are the responsibility of the Graduate Professional or their sponsoring institution. Key Responsibilities Under the supervision of the Programme Lead, the Graduate Professional will support both research and operational components of the ME WMDFZ Project. Duties include: 1. Research Support Conducting background research on disarmament, non‑proliferation, regional security, and Middle East affairs Synthesizing complex information into concise, high‑quality written briefs Supporting the development of analytical outputs and policy materials 2. Programme Implementation Assisting in planning and executing project activities such as: Meetings Workshops Conferences Field missions Coordinating logistics and supporting event documentation 3. Administrative and Operational Support Providing administrative assistance to project staff Managing communication with project partners and stakeholders Supporting internal coordination and project tracking 4. Additional Tasks Undertaking other responsibilities as assigned by project staff to ensure smooth programme delivery Required Skills and Interests Candidates should demonstrate strong academic and professional potential, including: Research and analytical skills Ability to produce clear, concise written outputs Strong organizational and multitasking abilities Excellent interpersonal and communication skills Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Interest in: International peace and security Disarmament Middle East regional affairs UN system and multilateral diplomacy Core UN Competencies Graduate Professionals are expected to embody the UN’s core values: Professionalism Integrity Respect for Diversity Additionally, they must demonstrate: Communication Clear drafting skills Ability to tailor communication to diverse audiences Openness in sharing information Teamwork Ability to collaborate with colleagues from diverse cultural backgrounds Strong interpersonal skills Planning and Organizing Efficient time management Ability to anticipate risks and adjust plans accordingly Creativity Willingness to propose new ideas and approaches Openness to innovation Client Orientation Understanding stakeholders’ needs Delivering work that reflects client perspectives Qualifications and Experience Education Requirements Applicants must meet one of the following: Be enrolled in a graduate programme (second university degree or higher) Have graduated with a relevant university degree and begin the programme within one year of graduation Hold a first or second university degree and begin the programme within one year of graduation Preferred fields include: International affairs Strategic or security studies International law Middle East studies Other related disciplines Work Experience No professional experience required Demonstrated interest or experience in non‑proliferation or arms control in the Middle East is an asset Must be computer literate Must show commitment to UN values and ability to work in multicultural environments Languages Fluency in English (oral and written) is required Knowledge of another UN language or a Middle Eastern language is an asset Deadline: 4 June 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Geneva time Important notes: Incomplete applications or those not submitted in PHP format will not be processed Applications generated with AI will not be accepted Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Women are strongly encouraged to apply Selected candidates must provide: Proof of valid medical insurance Medical certificate of good health Apply here For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here Discover more internship opportunities here The post United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research Graduate Professional Opportunity: Middle East WMD‑Free Zone Project (2026 Intake) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Research Assistant Short‑Term Contract Opportunity (July–December 2026) at Friedrich‑Ebert‑Stiftung South Africa
summary: The Friedrich‑Ebert‑Stiftung (FES) South Africa Office has announced an important short‑term employment opportunity for two skilled Research Assistants to support a major research and community‑engagement initiative focused on the just transition. This role is co‑funded by the European Union and implemented in partnership with SATRI, forming part of FES’s long‑standing commitment to advancing social democracy, gender justice, labour rights, and equitable development across Africa. FES, headquartered in Germany, has worked for more than 40 years to promote social democratic values such as solidarity, social justice, peace, and security throughout the African continent. Its South Africa Office contributes to this mission through political education, training programmes, public dialogues, action‑oriented research, publications, and policy consultation. The Research Assistant positions form part of this broader strategy to ensure that the voices of workers and communities are meaningfully integrated into national and international conversations on the just transition. About the Opportunity The Research Assistant (Short‑Term Contract: July–December 2026) will play a central role in gathering, analysing, and synthesizing information that supports worker‑ and community‑led approaches to the energy transition. The position is flexible and will require travel to Limpopo, Mpumalanga, and KwaZulu‑Natal between July and October 2026 for fieldwork and workshop participation. This opportunity is ideal for professionals with strong qualitative research skills, experience working with labour or community organisations, and a passion for gender‑just and socially inclusive policy development. Key Responsibilities The Research Assistants will support a wide range of research and engagement activities. Tasks include, but are not limited to: Secondary Information Reviews Conducting comprehensive reviews of existing literature, reports, and data related to the just transition, labour rights, gender justice, and community‑based energy transitions. Compilation of Synthesized Thematic Reports Producing structured, well‑analysed thematic reports based on secondary information sources, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and relevance to project objectives. Primary Data Collection and Facilitation Participating in fieldwork activities, including interviews, focus groups, and community workshops across Limpopo, Mpumalanga, and KZN. Documentation of Workshop Outcomes Recording, organising, and synthesizing workshop discussions, findings, and recommendations. Supporting the Translation of Community and Worker Voices Assisting in transforming qualitative insights into gender‑just demands and policy proposals that reflect the lived realities of workers and communities. Required Qualifications and Experience Applicants must demonstrate strong academic grounding, practical research experience, and familiarity with labour and community contexts. Requirements include: An Honours degree in Social Sciences, Development Studies, Public Policy, Labour Studies, Gender Studies, or related fields (a Master’s degree is an added advantage). Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English. Proficiency in local South African languages such as Setswana, Sepedi, or isiZulu is highly advantageous. At least 3 years of research experience, preferably using qualitative methods such as interviews, focus groups, and case studies. 2–3 years of experience in labour environments or working with trade unions, community organisations, or civil society groups. A general understanding of the energy transition, gender justice, and labour rights issues. Strong analytical, writing, and formal report‑compilation skills. Willingness to work flexible hours and travel for fieldwork (no vehicle required). Compensation The contract covers 132 working days between July and December 2026. A competitive daily rate will be offered based on the candidate’s experience and qualifications. FES is an equal‑opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. How to Apply Interested candidates must submit the following: A detailed CV including two traceable references A short cover letter outlining relevant experience and motivation Application Deadline: 08 June 2026 For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more job opportunities here The post Research Assistant Short‑Term Contract Opportunity (July–December 2026) at Friedrich‑Ebert‑Stiftung South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 2027–2029 EDGE Fellowship: A Transformative Global Opportunity for Early‑Career Conservation Leaders
summary: The Zoological Society of London (ZSL) has officially opened applications for the prestigious 2027–2029 EDGE Fellowship—one of the world’s most impactful capacity‑building programmes for early‑career conservationists dedicated to protecting Evolutionarily Distinct and Globally Endangered (EDGE) species. This fellowship is globally recognised for empowering emerging conservation leaders with the training, funding, and long‑term support needed to implement high‑impact, locally led conservation projects. The 2027 call introduces a structured two‑stage application process, beginning with an Expression of Interest (EOI). Shortlisted candidates will then be invited to submit a full application. This approach ensures that applicants can clearly articulate their conservation vision while demonstrating the local relevance and urgency of their proposed work. Understanding the EDGE Fellowship The EDGE Fellowship is a comprehensive, 28‑month leadership and conservation training programme designed to strengthen conservation action in regions of high biodiversity importance. It supports early‑career conservationists who are committed to safeguarding species that are both evolutionarily unique and globally threatened—species that often receive limited attention despite their ecological significance. From 2027 onward, each annual cohort will include at least 14 Fellows from countries outside the G12, the European Union, New Zealand, and South Korea. This ensures that conservation leadership is strengthened where it is most needed and where biodiversity loss is most acute. The fellowship is not simply a training initiative—it is a transformative leadership journey. Fellows gain hands‑on experience in project design, community engagement, scientific communication, budgeting, and long‑term conservation strategy development. They also join a global network of conservation practitioners working to protect some of the world’s most extraordinary and overlooked species. Eligibility of Species: Using the 2026 EDGE List Applicants must propose a project focused on an eligible species listed in the 2026 EDGE Species List , which must be downloaded and reviewed before completing the Expression of Interest form. Species are categorised into four priority levels: Higher Priority Medium Priority Lower Priority Ineligible While Higher Priority species will receive the strongest consideration, applicants focusing on Medium or Lower Priority species are still encouraged to apply—provided they can clearly justify the conservation importance and local relevance of their proposed work. Species classified as Ineligible do not appear in the EOI form and cannot be proposed for the 2027 Fellowship. Note: Species downlisted to NT or LC in the 2026.1 Red List (expected June/July) will not be eligible. Who Should Apply? The EDGE Fellowship seeks early‑career conservationists who meet the following criteria: Individuals committed to leading conservation action for an eligible EDGE species. Nationals or long‑term residents of the country where the project will take place. Applicants with strong ties to the specific region or landscape of the proposed project. Conservationists who can demonstrate the local need and urgency of their proposed work. Individuals ready to participate fully in training, mentorship, and peer‑learning activities. Applicants who wish to join a global network of conservation leaders dedicated to protecting unique and threatened species. What the Fellowship Offers Each EDGE Fellow will receive a comprehensive package of support over the 28‑month programme, including: Six weeks of in‑person training Support to develop a detailed and actionable conservation project plan A project implementation grant of GBP 15,000 Ongoing mentorship and professional development opportunities Access to a global network of conservation experts and peers This combination of funding, training, and long‑term support ensures that Fellows are equipped to deliver meaningful, lasting conservation outcomes. Application Timeline The 2027–2029 EDGE Fellowship follows a structured timeline: Stage Date Expression of Interest opens Wednesday 13 May 2026 Expression of Interest deadline 17:00 BST Wednesday 10 June 2026 Shortlisted applicants invited to submit full application Wednesday 8 July 2026 Full application deadline 17:00 BST Wednesday 12 August 2026 Interviews and final selection 14 September – 1 October 2026 Shortlisted applicants notified of outcome Week commencing 12 October 2026 2027 EDGE Fellowship period January 2027 – May 2029 How to Apply Applicants must: Download and review the 2026 EDGE Species List . Prepare their Expression of Interest with a clear focus on an eligible species. Submit the EOI through the official online form before the deadline. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post 2027–2029 EDGE Fellowship: A Transformative Global Opportunity for Early‑Career Conservation Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Africa Programme Officer at the International Detention Coalition (IDC): Full Job Details, Responsibilities, Requirements, and Application Guide
summary: The International Detention Coalition (IDC) is inviting applications for the position of Africa Programme Officer , a full‑time remote role based in Senegal . This opportunity is ideal for experienced professionals passionate about migration, human rights, advocacy, and strengthening civil society networks across the African continent. The application deadline is 10 June 2026 . About the International Detention Coalition (IDC) The International Detention Coalition is a global network of more than 200 civil society organisations and individuals across 75+ countries , working collectively to end immigration detention and promote rights‑based alternatives. IDC collaborates with partners on advocacy, research, capacity building, and coalition strengthening to advance humane, sustainable migration governance. Role Purpose The Africa Programme Officer will support IDC’s mission by combining strong advocacy expertise with excellent coordination and organizational skills. The role requires a professional who understands migration and human rights dynamics in Africa and can support regional programmes, stakeholder engagement, and member capacity development. The ideal candidate is proactive, detail‑oriented, and capable of managing multiple priorities in a remote environment while contributing meaningfully to IDC’s strategic goals. Role Summary Working closely with the Africa Regional Manager , the Programme Officer will: Support the design and implementation of regional programmes and advocacy strategies Coordinate events, consultations, and capacity‑building activities Strengthen networks with IDC members, partners, and decision‑makers Provide operational, administrative, research, and communications support Travel within the region when required This role is central to advancing IDC’s mission in Africa and ensuring effective collaboration across diverse stakeholders. Compensation Annual Salary: 12,000,000 – 16,000,000 CFA Employment Type: Full‑time Location: Remote (based in Senegal preferred) Detailed Responsibilities 1. Project Implementation, Administration, and Member Support Contribute to national and regional project implementation through technical, administrative, and logistical support Support IDC’s mission and strategic priorities through operational and advocacy engagement Assist in strengthening IDC’s network of members and partners across Africa Maintain organized project timelines, deliverables, and follow‑ups Support administrative and financial processes within the Africa Programme 2. Advocacy and Stakeholder Engagement Support the development and execution of advocacy strategies on immigration detention, alternatives to detention, and migrant/refugee rights Engage with government actors, civil society, UN agencies, and regional networks Draft and disseminate advocacy materials, briefing notes, and strategic communications Maintain regular contact with IDC members to identify policy gaps and advocacy opportunities Assist in planning and facilitating stakeholder consultations, trainings, and regional events 3. Communications Assist in developing print and digital communication materials for the region Support social media content creation and resource dissemination Monitor media platforms and track developments relevant to migration and detention in Africa 4. Internal Systems, Monitoring, Fundraising, and Institutional Support Contribute to fundraising through research, proposal drafting, and coordination Support monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) processes Maintain organized records of project outcomes and stakeholder engagement Assist in preparing reports for internal use, donors, and external audiences Support budget tracking and financial monitoring with relevant IDC staff 5. Core Responsibilities Contribute to a collaborative, supportive team culture Demonstrate alignment with IDC’s mission, vision, and values Uphold IDC’s safeguarding policies Engage with organizational systems to ensure sustainability and knowledge retention Key Selection Criteria Essential Requirements Strong understanding of African migration and human rights contexts Bachelor’s degree in international relations, law, migration studies, political science, or related fields Minimum five years of professional experience in project coordination, advocacy, and stakeholder engagement Experience with monitoring, evaluation, and reporting Strong writing, communication, and organizational skills Proven ability to work independently in a remote setting Experience engaging with civil society, government, and international organizations Ability to plan and facilitate events, workshops, and advocacy activities Excellent coordination skills and attention to detail Ability to manage competing deadlines and work flexibly Proficiency in digital communication tools and social media Ability to travel within the region Fluency in English and French (written and spoken) Strong commitment to IDC’s mission and safeguarding principles Application Process To apply: Prepare your resume Write a cover letter addressing all key selection criteria Upload both documents via the IDC Careers Webpage Application Deadline: 10 June 2026 Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion IDC strongly encourages applications from: Underrepresented groups Individuals with lived migration experience Candidates of all genders, races, ages, sexual orientations, religions, and abilities IDC is committed to ensuring an inclusive, equitable recruitment process and welcomes requests for reasonable accommodations. For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here Discover more job opportunities here The post Africa Programme Officer at the International Detention Coalition (IDC): Full Job Details, Responsibilities, Requirements, and Application Guide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Hokkaido University MEXT Scholarship 2027 in Japan: Fully Funded International Graduate Program (IGP) Opens for October 2027 Admission
summary: International students seeking a fully funded graduate degree in Japan now have an exciting opportunity to apply for the prestigious MEXT Scholarship International Graduate Program (IGP) Special Quota at Hokkaido University . The university has officially announced that applications for the October 2027 intake are now open, offering talented students from around the world the chance to pursue advanced research and graduate studies at one of Japan’s leading institutions. Funded by Japan’s Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT) , the scholarship supports outstanding international students who wish to undertake master s or doctoral studies through Hokkaido University s International Graduate Program (IGP). The scholarship provides an excellent opportunity for aspiring researchers to study in Japan, engage in cutting-edge scientific research, and collaborate with globally recognized academics while receiving comprehensive financial support. About Hokkaido University Hokkaido University is one of Japan’s most prestigious national universities, located in Sapporo . Known for its strong research culture, international outlook, and innovative academic programs, the university attracts students and researchers from around the world across a wide range of disciplines. Hokkaido University consistently ranks among Japan s leading research institutions and offers a vibrant academic environment that promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and global engagement. What is the MEXT Scholarship? The MEXT Scholarship is a prestigious fully funded scholarship sponsored by the Government of Japan through the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT). The program aims to: Promote international academic exchange Support global talent development Enhance research collaboration Strengthen educational partnerships between Japan and other countries Each year, selected international students receive financial support to pursue graduate studies at Japanese universities. The International Graduate Program (IGP) Special Quota at Hokkaido University is one of the pathways through which international students can access this scholarship. About the International Graduate Program (IGP) The International Graduate Program (IGP) at Hokkaido University is designed specifically for international students seeking advanced research training in English-based graduate programs. The IGP allows students to conduct research under the supervision of distinguished faculty members while pursuing master s or doctoral degrees. Participants gain access to: World-class research facilities International academic networks Interdisciplinary research opportunities Global career pathways Advanced scientific training Available Academic Divisions According to the university announcement, applicants can explore opportunities within the following affiliated programs: 1. Transdisciplinary Life Science Course Under the Division of Life Science, this course promotes interdisciplinary approaches to solving complex biological and life science challenges. Research areas may include: Molecular biology Genetics Biotechnology Environmental biology Biomedical sciences 2. Biosystems Science Course Also under the Division of Life Science, this course focuses on understanding biological systems and their applications to science and society. Potential research fields include: Agricultural sciences Biological systems engineering Ecology Sustainable resource management Life science innovation 3. Division of Soft Matter This division focuses on materials science and related interdisciplinary research. Areas of study may include: Soft materials Polymers Nanotechnology Material engineering Advanced manufacturing Why You Should Apply The MEXT Scholarship at Hokkaido University offers numerous academic and professional advantages. Key Benefits Include: Fully Funded Graduate Education Selected scholars typically receive comprehensive financial support from the Japanese Government. This often includes: Tuition coverage Monthly living stipend Travel support (subject to official MEXT regulations) Research support opportunities Applicants should review the official scholarship guidelines for complete benefit details. Access to World-Class Research Facilities Students will have opportunities to work in advanced laboratories and research environments while collaborating with internationally recognized experts. Study in Japan Japan is globally known for: Academic excellence Scientific innovation Technological advancement Cultural richness High quality of life The scholarship offers an opportunity to experience Japanese culture while pursuing graduate education. International Networking Students will join a diverse community of researchers and scholars from around the world, creating valuable academic and professional connections. Career Advancement Graduates benefit from: International credentials Research experience Global exposure Enhanced employability Opportunities in academia, industry, and international organizations Application Process Prospective applicants should begin preparing as early as possible. Step 1: Identify a Suitable Laboratory Applicants must first identify a laboratory that aligns with their academic interests and research goals. This is one of the most important parts of the application process. Students should carefully review available laboratories within the participating divisions and identify faculty members whose research aligns with their proposed study area. Step 2: Contact Laboratory Directors The university strongly encourages applicants to contact laboratory directors directly. Potential supervisors can provide information regarding: Application procedures Research opportunities Required documents Program-specific timelines Admission requirements Early communication is highly recommended. Step 3: Prepare Application Materials Applicants should follow the instructions provided by the laboratory and program coordinators regarding: Academic transcripts Research proposals Recommendation letters Language proficiency documentation Additional supporting materials Requirements may vary depending on the program and laboratory. Step 4: Submit Application Applicants must complete all required procedures according to the official schedule provided by the relevant laboratory and graduate program. Important Advice for Applicants To improve your chances of success: Start contacting potential supervisors early. Carefully study faculty research interests. Prepare a strong research proposal. Demonstrate alignment between your research goals and the laboratory’s expertise. Ensure all documents are submitted before internal deadlines. Competition for MEXT-funded positions is often very strong, making early preparation essential. Why Research Alignment Matters One of the most critical factors in the selection process is demonstrating that your research interests match the expertise of your prospective supervisor. Strong applicants typically: Understand current research trends in their field. Clearly articulate research objectives. Show how their work contributes to broader scientific knowledge. Explain why Hokkaido University is the ideal place to conduct their research. Application Status Applications Open The MEXT Scholarship International Graduate Program (October 2027 Admission) is currently accepting applications. Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin contacting laboratories immediately to obtain detailed application instructions and timelines. Apply Here For more information on this scholarship click here Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY here The post Hokkaido University MEXT Scholarship 2027 in Japan: Fully Funded International Graduate Program (IGP) Opens for October 2027 Admission appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Y+ Global Board Member 2026: Call for Young Leaders Living with HIV to Join a Global Advocacy Network
summary: Young leaders living with HIV now have a unique opportunity to influence global health policies, strengthen youth-led advocacy, and contribute to decision-making processes at the international level. The Global Network of Young People Living with HIV (Y+ Global) has officially opened applications for its 2026 Board Member positions , inviting passionate youth advocates from selected regions around the world to help guide the organization s strategic direction and global impact. As the world s largest global network of young people living with HIV, Y+ Global is seeking committed and experienced youth leaders who are ready to champion the rights, health, and well-being of young people living with and affected by HIV. Applications are open until 21 June 2026 . About Y+ Global Y+ Global is the world s largest network led by and for young people living with HIV. Founded in 2005, the organization works to ensure that young people living with and affected by HIV have meaningful opportunities to influence policies, programs, and decisions that affect their lives. Headquartered in both: The Hague Cape Town Y+ Global collaborates with over 109 implementing partners across 30 countries. The organization focuses on: HIV advocacy Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) Gender equality Human rights Youth leadership Capacity building Meaningful youth engagement Since 2024, Y+ Global has: Reached more than 102,000 young people through advocacy and empowerment programs. Positively impacted over 850,000 lives through its initiatives. What is the Y+ Global Board? The Y+ Global Board is responsible for providing strategic oversight, governance, accountability, and leadership for the organization. The Board consists of approximately: 8 to 10 young leaders All under the age of 30 Living with HIV Representing different regions worldwide Board members work collectively to strengthen the organization while ensuring diverse regional voices remain represented in global health discussions. Regions Currently Seeking Board Members Applications are specifically being sought from young leaders representing the following regions: Eastern Europe and Central Asia (EECA) West and Central Europe Middle East and North Africa (MENA) Latin America North America Applicants must belong to one of these regions to be eligible for this recruitment cycle. Why Join the Y+ Global Board? Serving on the Board provides an opportunity to contribute directly to international efforts aimed at improving the lives of young people living with HIV. Board members will help shape organizational priorities while participating in global conversations around health, human rights, and youth leadership. Benefits of Becoming a Y+ Global Board Member Although Board positions are voluntary, selected members gain significant professional and leadership opportunities. 1. Influence Global HIV and SRHR Responses Board members help ensure that the voices of young people living with and affected by HIV are represented in international decision-making spaces. 2. Expand Your Global Network Members connect with: Youth leaders HIV advocates Global health professionals Human rights experts Civil society organizations International development partners These relationships often lead to long-term collaboration and mentorship opportunities. 3. Develop Leadership Skills Board members strengthen their expertise in: Strategic planning Governance Policy advocacy Decision-making Organizational leadership These skills can significantly enhance future career and advocacy opportunities. 4. Represent Y+ Global Internationally Members may have opportunities to represent Y+ Global at: International conferences Regional forums Global meetings Advocacy events Participation depends on available funding and organizational priorities. 5. Access Capacity-Building Opportunities Selected members benefit from: Governance training Leadership development programs Advocacy workshops Technical learning opportunities These experiences help strengthen both personal and professional growth. 6. Gain Recognition as a Youth Leader Board service demonstrates leadership experience and commitment to social change, helping members build stronger profiles within the global health and development sectors. Key Responsibilities of Board Members Board members play an active role in ensuring the effectiveness and sustainability of the organization. Responsibilities include: Governance and Accountability Uphold ethical governance standards. Promote transparency and accountability. Support organizational integrity. Strategic Leadership Guide implementation of Y+ Global s strategic plan. Contribute to organizational priorities and decision-making. Board Participation Attend virtual and in-person Board meetings. Participate in working groups and committee activities. Policy Development Review and approve policies related to HIV, SRHR, and youth well-being. Provide strategic input on emerging issues. Regional Representation Engage with youth-led organizations in their regions. Share regional priorities and developments. Submit regular updates and reports. Advocacy and Representation Represent Y+ Global at relevant events and meetings. Support advocacy efforts and policy engagement. Secretariat Oversight Support recruitment and supervision of senior Secretariat members. Participate in annual governance reviews. Who Can Apply? To be eligible, applicants must meet all of the following criteria. Age Requirement Applicants must: Be under 30 years old at the time of application. HIV Status Applicants must: Be openly living with HIV. Nationality Requirement Applicants must: Be nationals or citizens of countries within the targeted regions. Language Requirement Applicants must: Demonstrate proficiency in English. Strong communication skills are essential for Board participation. Experience Requirement Applicants should have: Experience in HIV advocacy. Experience in Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) initiatives. Experience working in national or regional HIV responses. Experience with youth-led or youth-serving organizations. Personal Qualities Successful candidates should demonstrate: Commitment to youth empowerment. Passion for meaningful youth engagement. Cultural sensitivity. Inclusivity. Teamwork. Leadership potential. Board Term and Commitment Selected Board members will be expected to: Commit to a Two-Year Term The position requires dedication throughout the full Board cycle. Board members should be prepared to actively contribute to meetings, governance processes, advocacy initiatives, and strategic planning activities. Support Provided to Board Members To promote meaningful participation, Y+ Global provides several forms of support. Communications Stipend Board members receive support to cover: Internet costs Data expenses Communication needs related to Board duties Travel Support For approved official activities, Y+ Global may provide: Travel expenses Accommodation Per diem support Administrative Assistance Members receive practical support from the Y+ Global Secretariat to facilitate active participation. Selection Timeline Date Activity 27 May 2026 Applications Open 21 June 2026 Application Deadline 22 June – 3 July 2026 Application Review 6 July 2026 Shortlisting and Interviews 17 July 2026 Results Announcement How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application form before the deadline. Application Deadline 21 June 2026 Application Form Apply Here Contact Information For questions regarding the opportunity: Email: chair@yplusglobal.org For more information on this application click here Discover more opportunities on OFY, click here The post Y+ Global Board Member 2026: Call for Young Leaders Living with HIV to Join a Global Advocacy Network appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Union ( EU ) Youth Conference 2026: Call for Group Facilitators & Group Harvesters (Paid Roles) — Ireland’s EU Presidency
summary: Between 1 July and 31 December 2026, Ireland will hold the Presidency of the Council of the European Union. As part of this historic period, the National Youth Council of Ireland (NYCI), in partnership with the Department of Education and Youth, is preparing to host one of the most significant youth‑policy events of the year: the European Union Youth Conference (EUYC) , taking place in Dublin from 27–30 September 2026 . To support the delivery of this major international gathering, NYCI is inviting applications for 10 Group Facilitators and 10 Group Harvesters . These are paid roles , with travel, accommodation, and meals fully covered . Both roles are essential to ensuring meaningful youth participation, high‑quality dialogue, and strong policy outcomes aligned with EU Youth Goal #4: Information and Constructive Dialogue . Deadline to apply for both roles: Sunday, 7 June 2026 at 23:59 IST Overview of the EU Youth Conference 2026 The EU Youth Conference brings together young people, policymakers, youth workers, and experts from across Europe to shape the direction of EU youth policy. During Ireland’s EU Presidency, the conference will focus on strengthening access to reliable information, improving dialogue between young people and decision‑makers, and advancing youth participation across Europe. Participants will collaborate in structured workshops, discussions, and policy‑shaping sessions. The work of facilitators and harvesters will directly contribute to the EU Youth Dialogue process , influencing recommendations at the European level. Call for Group Facilitators NYCI is seeking 10 skilled Group Facilitators to lead and guide workshop groups throughout the conference. Role Purpose Facilitators will support young participants (18+), policymakers, and experts in exploring key issues related to Youth Goal #4. They will create inclusive spaces, encourage meaningful dialogue, and help groups develop clear, actionable recommendations. Key Responsibilities Lead structured workshop sessions during the EUYC Guide diverse groups through discussions and collaborative tasks Ensure inclusive, respectful, and productive dialogue Support participants in developing policy recommendations Attend required online and in‑person preparatory meetings Work on‑site in Dublin from 26–30 September 2026 Key Details Fee: €350 per day (approx. 6 days including preparation) Travel, accommodation, and meals provided Mandatory participation in preparatory meetings Work dates: Travel on 25 September; work from 26–30 September Who Should Apply Ideal candidates will have: Strong workshop facilitation and group‑management skills Experience working with diverse or international groups Interest in youth participation, youth policy, and international cooperation Further details are available in the official Facilitator Call document. For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here Call for Group Harvesters NYCI is also recruiting 10 Group Harvesters to document discussions and capture outcomes during the conference. Role Purpose Harvesters play a crucial role in ensuring that ideas, insights, and recommendations from each workshop are accurately recorded. Their work feeds directly into the EU Youth Dialogue reporting process. Key Responsibilities Capture workshop discussions in real time Summarize key points, recommendations, and outcomes Support facilitators in documenting group progress Prepare clear written outputs for EUYC reporting Participate in online and in‑person preparatory meetings Work on‑site in Dublin from 26–30 September 2026 Key Details Fee: €300 per day (approx. 5 days including preparation) Travel, accommodation, and meals provided Required participation in preparatory meetings Work dates: Travel on 25 September; work from 26–30 September Who Should Apply Ideal candidates will have: Strong notetaking and reporting skills Experience documenting workshops or group discussions Interest in youth policy, youth participation, and international work Further details are available in the official Harvester Call document. Application Requirements Applicants for both roles must confirm: They are available to travel on Friday, 25 September 2026 , and work in Dublin from Saturday 26 September to Wednesday 30 September 2026 . Applicants may also opt to join NYCI’s mailing list to receive updates about youth‑sector news and opportunities. For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here Deadline Applications for both roles must be submitted by Sunday, 7 June 2026 at 23:59 IST. Discover more opportunities here The post European Union ( EU ) Youth Conference 2026: Call for Group Facilitators Group Harvesters (Paid Roles) — Ireland’s EU Presidency appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
